people_highlights.name: Anthropic
people_highlights.post:
We're launching the Anthropic STEM Fellows Program. 

AI will accelerate progress in science and engineering. We're looking for experts across these fields to work alongside our research teams on specific projects over a few months.

Learn more and apply: https://t.co/MoF60j53pX

people_highlights.name: François Chollet
people_highlights.post: Human biological limits, like our tiny working memory and shallow calculation depth, are actually a feature. They force us to abstract, compress, intuit. If we had infinite resources, we would never have needed intelligence.
people_highlights.why: Recent post from Keras creator at Google

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post:
I find that open weights models over-perform on benchmarks compared to actual real-world usage, and Kimi feels like no exception.

For example, a small amount of use will show that Kimi is not as good as Claude Opus 4.6, which it beats on the benchmarks.

Still a good model, tho!
people_highlights.why: Recent post from AI researcher at Wharton

people_highlights.post: True to form, I've already seen OpenAI themselves refer to the new image model as "ChatGPT Images 2.0", "Image gen 2" and "gpt-image-2"

people_highlights.name: AWS
people_highlights.post:
A true grassroots community in Japan, built and run by its members.

Since 2010, the Japan AWS User Group (JAWS-UG) has been bringing builders together, even before AWS re:Invent and AWS Summit Tokyo. https://t.co/6Nv7dpZuet
